Hand-rolling these for a visual refresh is wasted effort and introduces accessibility regressions. 257→ 258→## Common Pitfalls 259→ 260→### Pitfall 1: Primary Color Becomes Blue But shadcn Defaults Assume Grayscale 261→**What goes wrong:** shadcn's default `--primary` is a near-black grayscale. Switching to blue (#2563EB) means hover states like `hover:bg-primary/90` produce a slightly transparent blue instead of a darker shade. Text contrast ratios may shift. 262→**Why it happens:** The oklch color space handles lightness differently than hex. 90% opacity of a saturated blue on white background looks different than 90% of near-black. 263→**How to avoid:** After setting --primary to blue oklch, verify all Button variants visually. If hover states look washed out, define an explicit `--primary-hover` or adjust the opacity value in button.tsx cva variants. 264→**Warning signs:** Buttons that look "faded" on hover rather than "pressed/darkened." 265→ 266→### Pitfall 2: Font Weight Mismatch Between Design and Code 267→**What goes wrong:** DM Sans loaded with only weights 400/500/600, but design uses `fontWeight: "bold"` (700) somewhere, causing the browser to synthesize bold from 600 -- looks slightly different. 268→**Why it happens:** Design files may use "bold" generically. DM Sans 600 (semibold) is the heaviest loaded weight. 269→**How to avoid:** Audit the .pen files for any fontWeight > 600 before implementation. If weight 700 appears, add it to the next/font/google config. From the design-system.pen data reviewed, all weights are 400/500/600 -- safe. 270→**Warning signs:** Text appearing slightly thicker or thinner than the design mockup. 271→ 272→### Pitfall 3: @theme inline Variables Not Picking Up New CSS Custom Properties 273→**What goes wrong:** Adding `--success` to `:root` but forgetting to add `--color-success: var(--success)` to the `@theme inline` block means `bg-success` Tailwind class won't work. 274→**Why it happens:** Tailwind v4 requires both the CSS custom property definition AND the @theme inline mapping for the utility to be generated. 275→**How to avoid:** For every new CSS variable added to `:root`, add the corresponding `--color-{name}: var(--{name})` to `@theme inline`. Verify with `bg-success` in a test element. 276→**Warning signs:** Tailwind classes like `bg-success` producing no styles (silently ignored). 277→ 278→### Pitfall 4: Admin Sidebar CSS Variable Scope Bleeding 279→**What goes wrong:** Overriding `--sidebar` variables globally affects the storefront sidebar/sheet components too. 280→**Why it happens:** If sidebar variables are set at `:root` level instead of scoped to the admin layout. 281→**How to avoid:** Scope admin sidebar overrides to the admin layout wrapper element using inline style or a CSS class selector like `.admin-layout { --sidebar: oklch(...); }`. 282→**Warning signs:** Storefront mobile drawer/sheet using dark navy colors instead of light theme. 283→ 284→### Pitfall 5: E2E Tests Breaking Due to Selector Changes 285→**What goes wrong:** Playwright E2E tests use selectors like `a[href*="/products/"]`, `getByRole('heading')`, and `getByPlaceholder(/search/i)`. If component structure changes, selectors break. 286→**Why it happens:** Visual rewrites can change DOM nesting, element types, or remove/rename elements. 287→**How to avoid:** Keep semantic HTML elements (headings, links, buttons, inputs). Add `data-testid` attributes if needed. After each screen rewrite, run relevant E2E tests if the Aspire stack is available. 288→**Warning signs:** E2E locators failing to find elements after component rewrite. 289→ 290→### Pitfall 6: Geist Font CSS Variable Still Referenced 291→**What goes wrong:** `--font-geist-sans` and `--font-geist-mono` still referenced in globals.css `@theme inline` after font swap, causing fallback to system fonts unexpectedly. 292→**Why it happens:** Incomplete cleanup of the old font configuration. 293→**How to avoid:** Search for all references to `geist` in the codebase and replace.    132→<frontmatter_guidance>
   133→**Purpose:** Enable automatic context assembly via dependency graph. Frontmatter makes summary metadata machine-readable so plan-phase can scan all summaries quickly and select relevant ones based on dependencies.

   135→**Fast scanning:** Frontmatter is first ~25 lines, cheap to scan across all summaries without reading full content.

   142→
   143→**Key-files:** Important files for @context references in PLAN.md.
   144→
   145→**Patterns:** Established conventions future phases should maintain.
   146→
   147→**Population:** Frontmatter is populated during summary creation in execute-plan.md. See `<step name="create_summary">` for field-by-field guidance.
   148→</frontmatter_guidance>

   151→The one-liner MUST be substantive:
   152→
   153→**Good:**
   154→- "JWT auth with refresh rotation using jose library"
   155→- "Prisma schema with User, Session, and Product models"
   156→- "Dashboard with real-time metrics via Server-Sent Events"
   157→
